Discipline

You learn leadership because clients need guidance

Almost 1 year of being a father  

HereвҖҷs a few things IвҖҷve learned 

Your relationship with your wife is beyond important, put her first and make her life as easy as possible

Because your child grows up in the world you create together, the better your relationship is, the better everything else tends to be 

Spending time with your daughter or son is the most productive thing you can do 

You will have less time for work and life in general but you will learn to be better and more efficient with the time you do have 

When you become a father, you have this constant proud feeling, you are building your family, you feel youвҖҷre on the right path 

You care less about status games and impressing others, you just want your wife and kids to think youвҖҷre cool so you prioritise them and stop chasing others, ironically by doing this other people will think youвҖҷre cooler 

From a fitness perspective, a lot of dads talk about fatherhood as if itвҖҷs the reason they got out of shape.

Sounds harsh, but in reality, most werenвҖҷt in great shape beforehand and they use fatherhood as an excuse, not good to blame family life, if anything it should motivate you 

Your kids are watching, your wife is watching

And one day your son or daughter will decide what normal looks like by looking at you, so set the standard 

Being a {{PERSON}}, healthy dad who decides his own schedule is the ultimate flex in life

Felt really good when I had 38km/h average after the first hour in today's training. When it was time to get back home I realized that it wasn't just my legs... #headwind
That's a good sunday: 6h spent riding on my own around black forest, some of the nicest roads there are. #cyclistsparadise #sundayride #soloadventure {{URL}}
Back in trainingmode! Preparation for second part of the season starts today. Got some big goals coming up. #toujoursplus #motivated
On another note: just sent in my last paper of this semester, which means I'm a free man for the next 2 months! #studentathlete #economics

A lot of suffering at the 6th round of the french cup yesterday. Managed to get from far back to the chase group in the last hour. Going into my midseason break with a good feeling and can't wait to prepare for the next goals! #sufferedlikeadog #12th

Good weekend of racing done with {{USER_MENTION}} . A lot of time spent at the front of the peloton defending our GC lead. Unfortunately we lost the jersey on the last stage but still a lot of lessons learned. #ToujoursPlus
Really excited for today's GP du canton d'Argovie in Gippingen. Thanks {{USER_MENTION}} for this opportunity to race with the big boys! #swisscyclingfamily
4th time Paris-Roubaix in a row for me. Let's hope for better luck this time around! {{URL}}
Wasn't such a bad idea to be in the breakaway at that point рҹҳ…. We oassed this climb 2 times after 30km of racing #crazystuff {{URL}}
Full gas all day after the bad stage of yesterday. Tried my luck in the breakaway, unfortunately already caught with 40km to go. Gonna sleep well tonight... #brumbrum #{{PERSON}}

The kindness I find most intriguing is self kindness. Until I started working as a {{PERSON}} had thought it was just me that had mean, cruel, abusive thinking. I now know itвҖҷs not. And self kindness is a way out. Practise being kind to you, as you would someone you truly love! {{URL}}
Did you know that studies have been down with children under two years old, and more than 85% of the time in situations, they will naturally be kind. It seems to be that we are made that way. {{URL}}
We started the month off yesterday looking at triggers and how to deal with them when they happen. {{LOCATION}} they do! Check the website for how to become a member for ВЈ1! {{URL}} {{URL}}
What if you tried that one for size this year? Not just as a new year resolution fad, but really said вҖҳyeah, IвҖҷm into altering my attitude to change my life.вҖҷ {{URL}}

Each year I take the time to complete the вҖҳend of year reviewвҖҷ. I created it years ago for clients, and those on my mailing list (if youвҖҷd like a copy DM the word REVIEW). Pause before you move into a new year and contemplate the past 12 months, what can you learn from that? {{URL}}

Regrets do not change the past. They do impact the present though. When you look to your past for what you can learn, itвҖҷs easier to move forward with strength. So take a moment to gather those lessons. x {{URL}}

It may be вҖҳjust another dayвҖҷ, but you can choose to play! What type of play brings lightness? Ideas below please, not everyone finds this easy. Play for me is dancing, playing with my dog, making things, painting and doing what I can when I can to be creative. What is it for you? {{URL}}
Take a deep breath. As you let it out feel what thatвҖҷs like in your body. Repeat, and again. Now you are here, what do you notice? {{URL}}
It really is! {{URL}}

{{USER_MENTION}} is 10! As we heard from the founder last night, it takes a team for a dream to come to be. And ten years on it's still going strong. I am honoured to volunteer and be able to share the night with such great women (and yes wear the same dress as a guest too!) рҹҳӮрҹҳӮ {{URL}}

One of my friends is an ex Olympian, she coaches up coming athletesвҖҰfor her fun IS being on the track in all weathers. For meвҖҰnot so much рҹҳӮ What is fun for you may not be fun for someone else, thatвҖҷs what is so wonderful about mankind, we are so varied. {{URL}}
What does doing it right mean for you? For me, itвҖҷs about honesty and being present with lifeвҖҰwhich actually takes more practise than it sounds! {{URL}}
Investing in you, imho, is one of the most wonderful things you can do. Be it learning a new craft, language, job, or going deeper spiritually. So rewarding. {{URL}}

What could your life be like if you made a few simple changes? This member shows up for all the calls & would even say herself that the changes have been subtle, but sustainable. Over time, many are seeing them and commenting on them. To become a member see link in bio. {{URL}}

I like this exploration as it seems to me that the opportunities donвҖҷt often come with a neon sign or big bow. Sometimes we need to look for them; what could be the opportunity in this for me? Even in those messy times, there are often gifts hiddenвҖҰletвҖҷs go treasure hunting! рҹҳҖ {{URL}}

Are you noticing the opportunities that are right in front of you? As is commonly said, if you want things to be different you need to do things differently...which could be taking that opportunity even if itвҖҷs a bit scary. (DM me if you want to chat more about this x) {{URL}}

I think about this often when reflecting on the year just past. What can I do now that I couldnвҖҷt at the start of the year. Who have I grown into due to the challenges, stretches and shoves that this year has provided? #robertbrault {{URL}}
It really is you know. And as August comes to a close, why not make a stance and look at adopting a {{PERSON}}, hopeful view of you! Go on, I dare ya! {{URL}}
I often ask in workshops what B.S. stands for. Most say Bull ShitвҖҰwhich is true. Funny how it also stands for belief system рҹҳҶ #justsaying {{URL}}

вҖҳIвҖҷm listening.вҖҷ Hmmm but are you? вҖҳIвҖҷve already told you.вҖҷ But was it clear? Communication is a big topic. When confusion happens, check, what did I think I heard versus what was actually said. Have we truly and clearly communicated? Or did the illusion just take place? {{URL}}

Much of the culture encourages us to be victim to circumstance. To blame others for how our life is. Does it help? Does it really change what is going on or where you want to be? вҖҰnah didnвҖҷt think so. {{URL}}
OR accept it and employ someone else to cover that base! {{URL}}

{{LOCATION}}. {{PERSON}} was a British teenager kidnapped into slavery in Ireland.

At about 16 years old, Irish raiders captured him and forced him to herd sheep alone in the wilderness for six years. Cold, hungry, and isolated, he turned to GodвҖ”praying constantly. He later wrote that his faith was forged in that suffering.

After a daring escape and return home, {{PERSON}} had a dream: the Irish people were calling him back. He could have stayed safe and remained bitter towards them for the wrong they had done to him. But insteadвҖҰ he went back to share the gospel.

Patrick spent the rest of his life preaching Christ across Ireland, {{LOCATION}}, {{LOCATION}}, and confronting pagan practices. Many credit his ministry with helping transform Ireland from widespread paganism to Christianity.

And the famous вҖңsnakesвҖқ he supposedly drove out? Ireland never had literal snakesвҖ”the story likely symbolizes the driving out of pagan worship and druid practices.

So St. PatrickвҖҷs Day isnвҖҷt really about luck, leprechauns, or green beer. ItвҖҷs about forgiveness, courage, and a former slave who returned to the land of his captivity to bring the message of Jesus.

A Letter to the Next Generation, and to the Mothers and Fathers of Faith:

In the {{NUMBER}}s, we began gathering young people on college campuses across {{LOCATION}}. We called these small weekend events Rock The NationsвҖ”simple gatherings with one purpose: to activate a generation and their leaders to take back America and their schools for Jesus.

As momentum grew, we knew God was calling us deeper. We launched PrayerStorm, where thousands of young people gathered to fast, pray, and cry out for America and the Church. None of us could have imagined that those seeds would begin to birth what we are now seeing across the United States.

PrayerStorm culminated in {{NUMBER}} with The Call on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. That day, nearly 800,000 students, parents, and leaders came together to fast, pray, and call down heaven for our nation. Every one of these gatherings was grounded in Malachi 4:5-6вҖ”the final passage of the Old Testament:

"See, I will send the prophet Elijah to you before that great and dreadful day of the Lord comes. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and the hearts of the children to their fathers."

We had seen something like this before. In the {{NUMBER}}s, the Jesus Movement swept across America, with thousands coming to Christ in miraculous ways. But the вҖңfathersвҖқ of the institutional church at the time didnвҖҷt like the disruption, the messiness, or the attentionвҖ”it was shut down too soon. A few, like {{PERSON}} and {{PERSON}}, recognized GodвҖҷs hand, but the conservative church of the day could not see past its discomfort.

Now, in this moment, with the assassination of Charlie Kirk, a generation has been jolted awake once again. The Spirit of Elijah is moving. {{PERSON}} people are risingвҖ”bold, {{LOCATION}}, courageous, and unwilling to back down. They will take back their schools, breathe life into dead churches, and carry Jesus into stadiums, cities, and nations.

But this time, it cannot be shut down. Why? Because they understand they need spiritual mothers and fathersвҖ”not to control them, not to silence them, but to walk beside them, cheer them on, and turn our hearts toward them as they turn theirs toward us. Together, we will see a movement that is not only powerful but generational.
The only revival in history that lasted beyond one generation was the Moravian Movement, which burned for 100 years. I believe we are on the brink of something even greater.

Are you ready? The 42nd generation is about to cross over. God is about to cover the whole earth with His glory.
